name: BREATH DIAL
conditioned_path: doomscrolling as a nervous-system coping loop — chasing the next card to feel in control
mechanic: A single dial sits center-screen; drag it clockwise to inhale (circle expands, tone rises), counterclockwise to exhale (circle contracts, tone falls). The dial moves only as fast as you drag — you set every pace. No wrong speed, no failure state. Five full slow cycles and the session softly closes.
video_trick: calm completion — the circle expanding and contracting is the whole reveal

name: ANCHOR HOLD
conditioned_path: the compulsive thumb-check — opening the app to feel grounded, landing in more chaos
mechanic: A glowing anchor sits on a dark sea. Hold your thumb on it: while held, the water calms, ripples still, and a progress arc fills slowly. Release early and the water stirs again; hold to the end of the arc and the screen settles to flat black with a soft chime. One hold, one resolution.
video_trick: calm completion — the stilling sea is the payoff

name: WORRY SORT
conditioned_path: the news-feed scan for reassurance — reading headlines to confirm the world is still manageable
mechanic: Soft text cards drift up labeled with abstract worry words. Tap each to gently place it into one of two jars: "in my control" or "not in my control." No wrong answers, no timer. When all cards are sorted, both jars seal with a quiet click and the screen rests. Control imposed on chaos.
video_trick: calm completion — both jars filling is the satisfying close

name: RIPPLE TAP
conditioned_path: the refresh lever — pulling to trigger a predictable response because unpredictability is unbearable
mechanic: A still water surface fills the screen. Tap anywhere and a ripple expands outward perfectly, predictably, always the same. Every tap produces the exact same beautiful ring. No variation, no surprise, no score. The nervous system learns: tap, ripple, safe. Tap as many times as you want; there is no end state.
video_trick: calm completion — the perfect predictable ripple is the hook

name: ORDER RESTORE
conditioned_path: the compulsive re-read of a thread to catch everything and feel nothing was missed
mechanic: A small grid of tiles is shuffled into disorder. Drag any tile to any open slot — all moves are reversible, nothing locks until you want it to. A gentle shimmer shows when the grid is fully ordered; tap DONE to close it. No timer, no wrong moves, no fail state. Restore it at your own pace.
video_trick: calm completion — the shimmer signaling order restored

name: RHYTHM TRACE
conditioned_path: the looping ASMR scroll where you replay the same soothing clip hoping the calm sticks this time
mechanic: A dotted line pulses across the screen in a slow, perfectly predictable wave. Trace it with your thumb — the line glows wherever you match it. There is no score, no gap penalty, no miss. The line repeats the same pattern indefinitely until you lift your thumb. Gentle control of a predictable rhythm.
video_trick: calm completion — the glowing trail you leave behind

name: SAFE LIST
conditioned_path: pinning saved posts as a security blanket — hoarding reassuring content to return to
mechanic: A blank list sits on screen with a soft prompt: "what do you know for certain right now?" Tap to add one soft tile at a time — each tile is a gentle color block, no words required. The list fills from the bottom. When you stop adding, tap CLOSE and the list folds away like a notebook. You made the list; you ended it.
video_trick: calm completion — closing the notebook is the ending ritual

name: SLOW POUR
conditioned_path: the pull-to-refresh lever used as a self-soothing gesture when nothing new helps but the gesture comforts
mechanic: Hold to tip a vessel; liquid pours at exactly the speed you tilt. Too fast and it splashes; too slow and it barely drips. The session is about finding the middle — a steady, predictable flow into the vessel below. When the vessel is full it chimes once and rests. Full control of the pour rate.
video_trick: calm completion — the vessel reaching full is the exhale

name: CLOUD NAME
conditioned_path: doomscrolling for reassurance — searching for named threats to contain them
mechanic: Soft clouds drift across the screen. Tap each cloud to name it with a one-tap label ("work", "health", "future"). Once named, the cloud turns a calm pastel and floats off the top of the screen. When the sky is empty, new clouds drift in slowly. You control which you name and when; none vanish until you name them.
video_trick: calm completion — a clear sky after the last cloud floats away

name: PULSE MATCH
conditioned_path: the news-refresh loop — checking for updates to synchronize inner state with outer reality
mechanic: A slow pulse glows at the center of the screen. Tap in time with it — not to beat it, just to match it. If you match for three pulses in a row the pulse slows one notch. Keep matching and keep slowing it until the pulse is barely visible and the screen dims to black. You decelerate the beat by following it.
video_trick: calm completion — the beat you slowed to stillness

name: FENCE WALK
conditioned_path: the predictable-feed craving — seeking a feed that only shows familiar things, no surprises
mechanic: A path stretches forward on a gentle loop; drag your thumb to walk it. The path always curves the same way, reveals the same soft scenery — a fence, a tree, a gate. Nothing unexpected ever appears. The walk has no destination and no end unless you lift your thumb. Predictable sensory input, fully controllable pace.
video_trick: calm completion — the reassuring sameness of the loop

name: UNDO STACK
conditioned_path: the re-read-before-send anxiety loop — drafting, deleting, redrafting because nothing feels safe to commit
mechanic: Soft action tiles stack up as you tap them. Every action is visible in a stack on the right. Swipe the stack to undo any step — every move is fully reversible in any order. There is no wrong sequence, no commit button unless you choose. The whole point is that nothing is permanent until you want it to be.
video_trick: calm completion — the satisfaction of knowing every step can be taken back

name: GROUND FIVE
conditioned_path: the sensory-scan doomscroll — scrolling to find something that makes the body feel present and safe
mechanic: Five sense prompts appear one at a time — SEE, HEAR, FEEL, SMELL, TASTE — each on a soft card. Tap the card to confirm you've noticed that sense right now. Each confirmed tap fills a gentle arc. When all five arcs are full the screen glows warmly for three seconds and closes. The 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique as a one-thumb session.
video_trick: calm completion — the final warm glow when all five senses are named

name: TIDE PULL
conditioned_path: the pull-to-refresh conditioned gesture — the swipe down that promises something new but delivers more chaos
mechanic: Pull down slowly and the tide rises up the screen; release and it ebbs back. The tide always behaves the same way — no variables, no surprise. Pull to bring it in, release to let it out. After six slow pull-release cycles the tide settles at a calm midline and the session ends with a soft wash sound. The gesture redeemed.
video_trick: calm completion — six tides and then the water settles

name: SOFT CHECK
conditioned_path: the compulsive notification check — opening the app to confirm everything is okay and closing having seen nothing that helps
mechanic: A single soft circle sits on screen labeled CHECK. Tap it once: a gentle "all clear" animation plays — a slow checkmark draws itself, a soft chime sounds, the circle pulses green. That is the whole session. One tap, one confirmation, done. The check is granted immediately, reliably, every time. No feed, no scroll, just the check.
video_trick: calm completion — the checkmark that actually resolves the loop

name: STORM CLEAR
conditioned_path: news-feed scanning for threats — the loop of checking to see if anything new is bad
mechanic: A storm of icon-sized threat words rains down the screen. Tap each to neutralize it — it dissolves into a soft ring. Clear them all before they pile up below the line. When the sky is empty a calm horizon fades in and your "cleared" count is your dare score. The anxiety discharge made finite and winnable.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the clear sky unlocking after the last word dissolves

name: CONTROL TOWER
conditioned_path: the god-complex feed-curation loop — long-pressing to teach the algorithm, seeking the fantasy of total feed control
mechanic: Inbound events stream across a radar screen. Tap each to reroute it safely before it reaches the center. Events are color-coded by type — predictable, fully legible, no surprises. Score is events routed without a center breach. The player is the air traffic controller — nothing happens without their approval.
video_trick: benchmark ego bait — "I cleared 47 without a breach"

name: SAFE COUNT
conditioned_path: the reassurance-seeking scroll — counting wins, good news, safe signals in a feed that keeps the alarm on
mechanic: Safe-signal tiles (soft green shapes) and threat tiles (soft red) drift down mixed together. Tap only the green ones to bank them before they reach the bottom. Miss a green and lose one from your tally; tap a red and your hand shakes for a second. Score is the highest safe-count you reach. Finding the safe signals and naming them — bounded, winnable.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — the almost-missed green tile that haunts the retry

name: KNOWN NEXT
conditioned_path: the story-bar scan — checking who posted so you know exactly what comes next before you watch
mechanic: A sequence of colored tiles is revealed one at a time in a known pattern. After four tiles the player must predict tile five by tapping a color. Correct: sequence continues and builds. Wrong: the predictable pattern is broken and the session ends. Score is the longest streak of correct predictions — comforted by knowing what comes next.
video_trick: participation-comment-bait — "call the next color before it flips"

name: PLAN THE DAY
conditioned_path: the productive-scroll loop — consuming planning content to feel prepared without actually committing to anything
mechanic: A blank day-grid of six soft time-blocks sits on screen. Drag a pre-made activity tile (rest, move, eat, connect, work, play) into any block. No wrong arrangement, no judgment. Swap tiles freely — all moves reversible. When you tap DONE the grid softly locks, the session closes, and the day has a shape. Control imposed on uncertainty.
video_trick: calm completion — the locked grid, the day with a visible shape
